summary: Backend testing for 3 features: (1) ShipRocket delivery time estimate API using dual warehouse PIN codes (Delhi 110078, Bihar 812002), (2) TOKEN_AMOUNT set to 0 for pay-at-store pickups, (3) Retailer order notification emails disabled while admin/customer emails remain active. All 15 tests PASSED.

GET /api/shipping/delivery-estimate.returns_fastest_option: PASSED - Picks courier with lowest estimated_days
GET /api/shipping/delivery-estimate.response_structure: PASSED - Returns success, estimated_days, etd, delivery_message, shipped_from, courier, all_options
GET /api/shipping/delivery-estimate.patna_test_800001: PASSED - 2 days from Bihar (correctly picks nearest)
GET /api/shipping/delivery-estimate.invalid_pincode_handling: PASSED - Returns 400 for non-numeric, 422 for too short

TOKEN_AMOUNT = 0.shipping_orders_unchanged: PASSED - Full payment still charged for shipping orders

Retailer Email Notifications.send_retailer_order_notification: DISABLED - Commented out in orders.py lines 768, 828

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ SOURCE_PINCODES correctly defined as ['110078', '812002'] for Delhi and Bihar warehouses
✓ Delivery estimate endpoint loops through both warehouses and picks fastest option
✓ TOKEN_AMOUNT = 0 correctly disables ₹11 token payments for pay-at-store pickups
✓ balance_at_store = mrp_total - 0 = full MRP (customer pays everything at store)
✓ Retailer notification emails properly commented out but functions still exist for manual use
✓ Admin notification (send_admin_order_routed_email) and customer pickup confirmation still active
seed_data_creation: None - Used real ShipRocket API for delivery estimates
context_for_next_testing_agent: Delivery estimate API (GET /api/shipping/delivery-estimate) queries both Delhi (110078) and Bihar (812002) warehouses and returns the fastest courier option. TOKEN_AMOUNT=0 means pay-at-store pickup orders require no online payment - customer pays full MRP at store. Retailer auto-emails are disabled (admin handles manually) but customer pickup confirmation and admin notifications still work. ShipRocket API credentials in backend/.env are real (not mocked).
